requirement_text: RTI shall provide the Connect service with federate ambassador, callback model, and optional local settings designator inputs.
notes: Paraphrased engineering split of the coarse 4.2 service row.

requirement_text: Connect shall be rejected when the RTI ambassador is already connected.
notes: Current backend raises AlreadyConnected.

requirement_text: Successful Connect shall bind the ambassador, persist the selected callback model, persist the optional local settings designator, and mark the ambassador connected.
notes: Direct lifecycle witness now asserts bound ambassador state selected callback model persisted local settings designator and connected state before any join occurs.

requirement_text: Connect shall establish the callback-delivery model that governs subsequent RTI callback behavior for the connected ambassador.
notes: Directly contrasts evoked and immediate callback delivery on the same follow-on federation report service.

requirement_text: Connect shall report an AlreadyConnected error for a repeated connection attempt on the same ambassador.
notes: Explicit exception family captured for harmonized requirement slicing.

requirement_text: Connection state relevant to later federation participation should be representable through MOM-visible federate state once the connected ambassador joins a federation.
notes: Once connected ambassadors join the federation their MOM federate objects are visible through the observer summary.

requirement_text: Connect shall be covered by direct service tests, with hosted gRPC and REST replay recorded separately as backend-resolution evidence where available.
notes: The direct Python 2010 lane is proven directly; hosted gRPC and REST lifecycle replay is recorded separately in backend-resolution evidence.

requirement_text: RTI shall provide the Disconnect service.
notes: Coarse 4.3 row split into implementation-driving subrequirements.

requirement_text: Disconnect shall require a connected ambassador that is no longer a federation execution member.
notes: The current backend rejects disconnect while joined.

requirement_text: Successful Disconnect shall clear connected state, clear the bound ambassador reference, and clear queued callbacks for the disconnected ambassador.
notes: Directly asserts buffered callback queue cleanup during disconnect.

requirement_text: After Disconnect, the RTI shall cease callback delivery to the disconnected ambassador.
notes: Directly asserts disconnect stops delivery of buffered report callbacks by clearing the pending queue.

requirement_text: Disconnect shall report FederateIsExecutionMember when a joined federate attempts to disconnect before resigning.
notes: The connected-but-still-joined guard is implemented and tested.

requirement_text: Disconnect should leave no active MOM-visible federate session associated with the disconnected ambassador.
notes: Shared disconnect-MOM cleanup scenarios now prove the disconnected ambassador no longer has an active MOM-visible federate session.

requirement_text: Disconnect shall be covered by direct service tests, with hosted gRPC and REST replay recorded separately as backend-resolution evidence where available.
notes: The direct Python 2010 lane is proven directly; hosted gRPC and REST lifecycle replay is recorded separately in backend-resolution evidence.

requirement_text: RTI shall provide the Create Federation Execution service, including the explicit-MIM overload shape used by the current API surface.
notes: The current implementation exposes both create variants.

requirement_text: Create Federation Execution shall require a connected ambassador and shall reject duplicate federation execution names.
notes: NotConnected and duplicate-name behavior are both covered in the current suite.

requirement_text: Successful Create Federation Execution shall install the federation, resolve and merge the supplied FOM modules, supply the standard MIM by default when needed, and choose a logical time factory compatible with the merged model.
notes: Direct lifecycle witness now asserts federation installation merged FOM-module retention automatic MIM selection and logical-time-factory selection before a federate joins.

requirement_text: Create Federation Execution shall make the created federation observable to subsequent callback-based reporting services such as Report Federation Executions.
notes: This is the first observable callback consequence of successful creation in the current repo.

requirement_text: Create Federation Execution shall distinguish duplicate-name, FOM-open, FOM-read, MIM-open, MIM-read, invalid-time-factory, inconsistent-FOM, and invalid-standard-MIM-designator failures.
notes: Current tests already separate the major exception families instead of collapsing them into one generic failure.

requirement_text: Successful Create Federation Execution shall expose the standard MIM-backed federation object model so MOM federation classes and interactions are available once a federate joins.
notes: MOM visibility is currently proven through post-join observation of the created federation.

requirement_text: Create Federation Execution shall be covered by direct service tests, with hosted gRPC and REST replay recorded separately as backend-resolution evidence where available.
notes: The direct Python 2010 lane is proven directly; hosted gRPC and REST lifecycle replay is recorded separately in backend-resolution evidence.

requirement_text: RTI shall provide the Destroy Federation Execution service.
notes: The coarse 4.6 row is now split into distinct behavioral slices.

requirement_text: Destroy Federation Execution shall require a connected ambassador and a target federation execution that has no currently joined federates.
notes: Missing-federation and joined-federates guards are both implemented.

requirement_text: Successful Destroy Federation Execution shall remove the target federation execution from the RTI federation catalog.
notes: The current test asserts the federation record is removed from engine state.

requirement_text: After successful destruction, callback-based federation listing shall no longer report the destroyed federation execution.
notes: Directly asserts destroyed federations disappear from subsequent Report Federation Executions callbacks.

requirement_text: Destroy Federation Execution shall report FederatesCurrentlyJoined when members remain and FederationExecutionDoesNotExist when the target federation is unknown.
notes: The two principal destroy guards are already separated in the test suite.

requirement_text: Successful destruction should remove the destroyed federation from subsequent MOM-visible federation state and reports.
notes: Subsequent federation-execution reporting no longer includes the destroyed federation as observer-facing state.

requirement_text: Destroy Federation Execution shall be covered by direct service tests, with hosted gRPC and REST replay recorded separately as backend-resolution evidence where available.
notes: The direct Python 2010 lane is proven directly; hosted gRPC and REST lifecycle replay is recorded separately in backend-resolution evidence.

requirement_text: RTI shall provide the Join Federation Execution service, including the overload shapes that accept generated federate names and optional additional FOM modules.
notes: Current Python API supports the common join call shapes used by the backend.

requirement_text: Join Federation Execution shall require a connected ambassador that is not already joined, an existing federation execution, a non-conflicting federate name, and no save or restore already in progress for the federation.
notes: The repo already exercises the main join preconditions through dedicated negative tests.

requirement_text: Successful Join Federation Execution shall allocate a federate handle, bind the federate to the target federation, initialize logical time and lookahead state, merge any accepted additional FOM modules, and register the federate in the federation state.
notes: Direct lifecycle witness now asserts joined handle allocation federation binding initial logical time and lookahead additional FOM-module merge and federation membership registration.

requirement_text: Successful Join Federation Execution shall trigger delivery of any open synchronization-point announcements relevant to the joiner and enable subsequent callback delivery for joined participation.
notes: Late-join synchronization announcement behavior is already verified.

requirement_text: Join Federation Execution shall distinguish not-connected, missing-federation, duplicate-federate-name, already-joined, save-in-progress, restore-in-progress, FOM-open, FOM-read, invalid-time-factory, and inconsistent-FOM failures.
notes: The current suite already treats join failures as distinct exception requirements.

requirement_text: Successful Join Federation Execution shall create or refresh the joined federate's MOM-visible federate object and refresh MOM federation state.
notes: MOM federate-object creation and joined-state visibility are already observable.

requirement_text: Join Federation Execution shall be covered by direct service tests, with hosted gRPC and REST replay recorded separately as backend-resolution evidence where available.
notes: The direct Python 2010 lane is proven directly; hosted gRPC and REST lifecycle replay is recorded separately in backend-resolution evidence.

requirement_text: RTI shall provide the Resign Federation Execution service.
notes: The coarse resign row is decomposed into subrequirements for implementation and evidence.

requirement_text: Resign Federation Execution shall require a connected ambassador that is currently joined and shall validate ownership and pending-acquisition conditions against the selected resign action.
notes: The current backend enforces joined-state, ownership, and acquisition preconditions.

requirement_text: Successful Resign Federation Execution shall remove the federate from federation membership, remove or divest owned objects as directed by the resign action, remove synchronization-point participation, refresh time advancement processing, and clear local publication and subscription state.
notes: Direct lifecycle witness now asserts resign removes federation membership deletes owned objects for the selected directive drops synchronization-point participation processes a blocked time advance and clears local declaration and region state.

requirement_text: After successful resignation, the resigned federate shall no longer receive federation-participation callbacks as a joined member.
notes: Directly asserts a resigned federate stops receiving subsequent federation save callbacks while remaining connected.

requirement_text: Resign Federation Execution shall distinguish not-connected, not-joined, invalid-resign-action, owns-attributes, and ownership-acquisition-pending failures.
notes: The negative-path suite already covers the principal resign exception families.

requirement_text: Successful Resign Federation Execution shall remove the resigned federate's MOM federate object and refresh MOM federation state for the remaining federation members.
notes: Directly asserts resign removes the MOM federate object and refreshes observer-visible MOM summary state.

requirement_text: Resign Federation Execution shall be covered by direct service tests, with hosted gRPC and REST replay recorded separately as backend-resolution evidence where available.
notes: The direct Python 2010 lane is proven directly; hosted gRPC and REST lifecycle replay is recorded separately in backend-resolution evidence.
